title: reseda undata var. leucantha
intro: Reseda undata var. leucantha, also known as White Moly, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family and is characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and distinctive, hairless leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of White Moly include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and act as a natural decongestant, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. White Moly is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
